steps.title: Spot the cause-and-effect relationship
steps.content: The first sentence establishes a problem—gold deposits are too deeply buried for individual prospectors to reach. The second sentence describes what happened by 1852: prospectors gave up their individual dreams and went to work for large companies. The transition must show this consequence.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Consequently marks causation: by 1852, because prospectors could not reach the deep gold alone, they gave up and joined large companies.

The other three miss this causal link: Furthermore merely adds information, Still contradicts by suggesting persistence despite the problem, and Next presents events as sequential without cause.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
